Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant'S Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Admissions, Set #2, Form Interrogatory 17.1 And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-19-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Kathryn M. Mente, appeared for Plaintiff/moving party
Wayne D. Clayton, appeared for Defendnt/opposing party via telephone

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Defendant's Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Admissions, Set 2, Form Interrogatory 17.1 is DENIED. The court does not find that there is good cause to allow more than 35 Requests for Admissions. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Poins And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Defendant'S Responses To Plinatiff'S Request For Admissions, Set #2, Form Interrog 17.1, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-16-2005 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant'S Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Admissions,Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-29-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCE: Kathryn M, Mente for Plaintiff/Moving Party
Wayne D. Clayton for Defendant/Opposing Party (telephonic appearance)


RULING: Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted - Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Defendant's Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Admissions, Set One, is granted. Defendant's objections are overruled. Defendant is ordered to respond further within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion Fro Mental Examination Of Plft Artit Thamok
Off Calendar Nov-29-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:



APPEARANCE: Kathryn M, Mente for Defendant/Opposing Party
Wayne D. Clayton for Plaintiff/Moving Party (telephonic appearance)


RULING: Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted - (1) Defendant's Motion For Mental Examination of Plaintiff is denied upon Plaintiff's stipulation that Plaintiff is making no claim for mental and emotional distress over and above that usually associated with the injury claimed and that no expert testimony regarding Plaintiff's mental and emotional distress will be presented at trial in support of the claim for damages.
The court finds that the motion was made without substantial justification. Sanctions are imposed against Defendant in the amount of $1,400.00 to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Oct-20-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Kathryn M. Mente, appeared for Plaintiff
No other appearance.

Ruling:
Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Defendant's Response To Plaintiff's Request For Production of Documents, Set No. 2, Nos. 58-61 is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to Nos. 58 and 59, limited the years 2003 and 2004 as the documents are relevant to the San Francisco branch's growth rate. The motion is granted as to Nos. 60 and 61, limited to the years 2003 and 2004. Responses to Nos. 58 and 59 shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Responses to Nos. 60 and 61 shall be made within 30 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-13-2005 Set For Trial On Jan-23-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jan-04-06 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 602

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2005 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Wayne D. Clayton, Esq. of HOROWITZ & CLAYTON 310/442-1122, appearing for defendant/moving party Auto-Chlor System of Northern California Inc.
Kathryn M. Mente, Esq. of McCORMAC & ASSOCIATES 399-1722, appearing for plaintiff/opposing party Artit Thamok

RULING:

Re- Notice Of Motion To Compel Response To Request For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-28-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF ARTIT THAMOK'S Motion to compel further response to request for production of documents and documents is denied. Plaintiff has failed to comply with Civil Procedure Code Section 1985.6 with regard to employees or former employees. Further, request for production of documents regarding individuals who were interviewed but not hired is, on the record before the court, not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, nor has plaintiff shown a compelling need for such information and that the information cannot reasonably be obtained through depositions or nonconfidential sources. Requests for sanctions are denied. (622/M. MILLER)
